This record has been sitting on the shelf for quite a while now, and I never really took the time to give it a proper listen. It’s been one of those forgotten records tucked away in my collection.

Last week, I decided to move my record collection to the living room, giving me a fresh perspective on the records I hadn’t played in ages. It felt like the perfect time to listen some of those forgotten records and rediscover why it's in my collection in the first place.

I can't say much about the record itself... There’s no label or artist to research, which only adds to the mystery. Yet, despite the anonymity, I was pleasantly surprised by the vibe it gave off. In addition to the sensual vocals and acid lines, there’s a certain peak-time energy that builds throughout the track—one that makes it easy to get those hands up in the air when the drop hits

From a state steeped in house culture, Harry Romero is one of New Jersey’s finest house exports. Emerging in the mid 1990s and remaining one of the genre’s most in demand producers and remixers to this day, he now debuts on the legendary Nu Groove in the first wave of artists to release music for the new era of the label. Delivering exquisite two tracker ‘I Love / The City’, Harry plays tribute to classics from the Nu Groove catalogue with his modern ‘Choo Choo’ twist he’s loved and revered for. House bubbler ‘I Love’ opens the package with depth and warmth to get you in the mood, while ‘The City’ is a little busier in its composition, a spoken-word vocal telling the familiar tale of house music’s ability to unite, while kinetic percussion and glowy synths make this another striking cut.
